```text
1	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
2	<ModMetadata xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
3	  <Name>Network Fix</Name>
4	  <Author>FlorpyDorp</Author>
5	  <Version>1.0.0</Version>
6	  <Description>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx to be installed with the StationeersLaunchPad plugin.
7	See: https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad
8	
9	Client-side fixes for netcode bugs that cause severe desync on high-jitter long-distance connections (e.g. Los Angeles to New Zealand servers). Does not affect server behaviour.
10	
11	Fix 1 — Fragment Overflow Prevention:
12	FragmentHandler.Receive() copies bytes into a fixed-size buffer without bounds-checking before each write. On high-jitter routes, out-of-order packets from different ticks mix together, overflowing the buffer and throwing an IndexOutOfRangeException that leaves the reassembly permanently stalled until disconnect. This fix detects the pre-overflow condition in a Harmony prefix and resets cleanly before the corruption occurs.
13	
14	Fix 2 — Fragment Stall Recovery:
15	If fragment assembly starts but no new bytes arrive for 3 seconds (dropped final fragment, bad size header, etc.), the partial buffer is discarded and the system resets to accept a fresh header packet. Vanilla behaviour leaves the client permanently mid-assembly until it times out.
16	
17	Fix 3 — Smooth Time Sync:
18	The server broadcasts the authoritative game clock every 60 seconds. Vanilla UpdateServerTimeOffset() hard-jumps the client time offset instantly, causing visible physics and interpolation artifacts. This fix captures the target offset and lerps to it at 3 seconds per second of correction, eliminating the jitter. Jumps larger than 10 seconds (first join, server restart) still snap immediately.
19	
20	Press F9 in-game to toggle the network diagnostics overlay showing RTT, time offset, assembly progress, and per-fix reset counters.
21	
22	Safe to use when joining any server. Client-only — no server-side changes required.</Description>
23	</ModMetadata>
24
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
2	<ModMetadata xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
3	  <Name>AIMeE Bug Fixes</Name>
4	  <Author>FlorpyDorp</Author>
5	  <Version>0.15.3</Version>
6	  <WorkshopHandle>3674960841</WorkshopHandle>
7	  <Description>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx to be installed with the StationeersLaunchPad plugin.
8	See: https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad
9	
10	Fixes fourteen bugs with the AIMeE mining robot:
11	
12	1. Mine Deadlock: AIMeE stops mining after first ore because _mineCancellation is never reset after Mine() completes.
13	2. Off-State Teleporting: When turned off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports because TryUnstuck/PathToTarget have no OnOff guard.
14	3. Tread Animation Desync: On dedicated server clients, tread UV animations are frozen because WheelRpm is never set on non-authority clients.
15	4. Roam Regression: AIMeE stops exploring when no ore is in scan range because driving logic is skipped when queue is empty. Also restores MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32.
16	5. Pickup Launch: Picking up/dragging AIMeE launches the player because wheel motor torque transfers through the drag joint.
17	6. MineablesInQueue Client Sync: MineablesInQueue always reads 0 on multiplayer clients because the queue is a local server-only list.
18	7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount: Vicinity count includes ore from entire veins beyond the search area due to missing per-ore bounds check.
19	8. Carry Collision Launch: Carrying AIMeE and bumping terrain/objects launches the player because WheelCollider suspension forces transfer through the drag joint.
20	9. Stuck Teleporting: When stuck for 60 seconds, Unstuck() teleports AIMeE by directly writing position. Replaced with physics-based escape (backward impulse + random torque). Also adds velocity-based stuck detection to catch AIMeEs pushing against each other.
21	10. Distant Ore Selection: AIMeEs mine far-away ore instead of closest. Queue now sorted by distance so nearest ore is mined first.
22	11. Circling Fix: AIMeEs circle aimlessly due to stuck detection feedback loop. Added post-reverse cooldown, mild steering reset after reversing, and reduced reverse duration. Also reduced queue cap from 10 to 5 for better multi-AIMeE ore sharing.
23	12. Ghost Ore Fix: Ore mined by AIMeE stays visible on multiplayer clients. Fixed by injecting a VoxelChangeEvent directly into the SyncList, bypassing SetDensityWorldSpace entirely. Zero server-side terrain work. Client receives the event and calls MineAtPositionClient to remove the ore. Works for all surface ore visible to players.
24	13. Underground Ore Fix: AIMeE targets ore deep underground in tunnels and caves. The surface check (IsNearSurface) only tests a single voxel 3 blocks above the ore. In a tunnel, that point is empty, so underground ore passes the check. Fixed with a column scan that detects cave ceilings — if solid terrain exists above an empty gap, the ore is rejected as unreachable.
25	14. Stale Queue Fix: AIMeE's ore queue is never cleared when picked up, turned on, or given a TargetXYZ. Stale entries point to ore at old locations and block other AIMeEs via AllAimeeQueuedMinables. Fixed by clearing the queue on pickup, power-on, and TargetX/Y/Z writes.
26	
27	Debug overlay now shows [SERVER]/[REMOTE] labels and displays client-appropriate data on dedicated servers.
28	
29	Works on both dedicated servers and clients.</Description>
30	
31	  <ChangeLog><![CDATA[
32	v0.15.3: Bug 12 improved — two-event approach (no terrain holes)
33	- Bug 12: Previous fix (density 0) carved visible holes in terrain on clients
34	- Now uses two VoxelChangeEvents per mine: event 1 (density 0) removes ore,
35	  event 2 (original density) restores terrain. Ore gone, terrain intact.
36	- Server terrain still completely unmodified
37	
38	v0.15.2: Bug 12 improved — fix ghost ore for subsurface mining
39	- Bug 12: Send density 0 in VoxelChangeEvent instead of current density
40	- Ore 1-3 blocks below surface has solid density (>= 127), which failed ShouldReleaseMinables
41	- MineAtPositionClient never fired on clients — ghost ore persisted
42	- Now always sends density 0: client carves terrain + removes ore model (same as player mining)
43	- Server terrain unmodified — only SyncList is touched
44	- Client visual hole is cosmetic, resets on reconnect
45	
46	v0.15.1: Bug 13 improved — increased cave scan height
47	- Bug 13: Increased IsNearSurface scan height from 20 to 64 blocks
48	- Tall caves (17+ blocks) were fooling the scan — ceiling not found within 20 blocks
49	- 64 blocks covers all realistic Stationeers terrain depths
50	- Ore in tall natural caves and player-dug caverns now correctly rejected
51	
52	v0.15.0: Bug 14 — Stale queue fix
53	- Bug 14: Queue now clears when AIMeE is picked up, turned on, or given TargetXYZ
54	- Prevents stale ore entries pointing to old locations after relocation
55	- Frees AllAimeeQueuedMinables hash entries so other AIMeEs can claim ore
56	- Detected via UpdateEachFrame state tracking (IsCursor, OnOff transitions)
57	- TargetX/Y/Z writes detected via SetLogicValue postfix
58	
59	v0.14.0: Bug 12 improved — direct SyncList injection
60	- Bug 12: Replaced density-passthrough with direct VoxelChangeEvent SyncList injection
61	- Bypasses SetDensityWorldSpace entirely — zero server-side terrain work
62	- No octree modification, no UpdateCanAirPass, no DirtyLods on server
63	- Client receives event, calls MineAtPositionClient — ore model disappears
64	- Cleaner, faster, and no voxel density changes anywhere
65	
66	v0.13.0: Bug 12 proper fix — density-passthrough approach
67	- Bug 12: Ghost ore now disappears on clients WITHOUT modifying terrain
68	- Reads current voxel density at ore position and re-sets it to same value
69	- Creates VoxelChangeEvent (client sync) without changing any terrain
70	- For surface ore (density < 50%): sync triggers MineAtPositionClient on clients
71	- For buried ore (density >= 50%): invisible to players, self-heals on terrain mining or rejoin
72	- No more terrain destruction from v0.12.0
73	
74	v0.12.2: CRITICAL — Remove all terrain voxel modification
75	- Removed ALL SetDensityWorldSpace calls — AIMeE must never modify terrain
76	- Bug 12 (ghost ore) patch DISABLED — the game's client sync mechanism (VoxelChangeEvent) inherently modifies terrain density, which destroys the ground under/around ore
77	- AIMeE only picks up surface ore via TryMineServer, no terrain digging
78	- Ghost ore on clients is cosmetic; terrain destruction is gameplay-breaking
79	
80	v0.12.1: Cliff-climbing regression fix
81	- Fix: AIMeE can climb steep terrain again — velocity-based stuck detection now also checks position delta
82	- Stuck detection only triggers reverse if position moved < 0.5m over 5 seconds (rules out slow climbing)
83	- Previously, climbing a cliff at low velocity falsely triggered stuck→reverse loop every 5 seconds
84	
85	v0.12.0: Bug 12 re-fix + Roam distance improvement
86	- Bug 12: Multi-pronged ghost ore fix — calls SetDensityWorldSpace in OnMinedOre postfix (catches all mining paths) AND before TryMineServer in Roam prefix (matches Quarry pattern)
87	- Bug 12: TryMineServer postfix kept as belt-and-suspenders with unconditional logging
88	- Bug 12: Uses TargetMinable position via GetMinableWorldPosition for correct voxel coords
89	- Roam: AIMeE now drives straight for 8-12 seconds before turning (was 1-3s)
90	- Roam: Steering angle kept at ±30° for good coverage when turning
91	
92	v0.11.0: Bug 12 + Bug 13 — Ghost ore fix + underground ore fix
93	- Bug 12: Ore mined by AIMeE now correctly disappears on multiplayer clients
94	- Bug 12: Postfix on Vein.TryMineServer adds SetDensityWorldSpace(0) for VoxelChangeEvent client sync
95	- Bug 13: AIMeE no longer targets ore deep underground in tunnels/caves
96	- Bug 13: Replaced single-voxel surface check with column scan that detects cave ceilings
97	- Bug 13: Scans up to 20 blocks above ore; rejects if solid→empty→solid pattern found
98	- Bug 7: Vicinity count now also uses improved Bug 13 surface check
99	
100	v0.10.0: Bug 12 — Ghost ore client sync fix
101	- Bug 12: Ore mined by AIMeE now correctly disappears on multiplayer clients
102	- Bug 12: Postfix on Vein.TryMineServer adds SetDensityWorldSpace(0) to create VoxelChangeEvent for SyncList client sync
103	- Bug 12: Also fixes the same base game bug in the original Roam() and async Mine() paths
104	
105	v0.9.0: Bug 11 — Circling fix + debug overlay client/server awareness
106	- Bug 11: Add post-reverse cooldown (3s) to break stuck→reverse→stuck feedback loops
107	- Bug 11: Reset steering to mild angle (±5°) when reverse ends (was keeping ±30°)
108	- Bug 11: Increase stuck detection time from 3s to 5s (less aggressive triggering)
109	- Bug 11: Reduce velocity-stuck reverse duration from 5s to 3s
110	- Bug 9: Reduce backward impulse from 2.0 to 1.0 (prevents wheelies)
111	- Bug 9: Reduce random torque from ±3 to ±2 (less violent spin)
112	- Bug 9: Reduce Unstuck reverse time from 8s to 5s
113	- Queue cap reduced from 10 to 5 per AIMeE (better multi-AIMeE ore sharing)
114	- Debug overlay now shows [SERVER] or [REMOTE] label per AIMeE
115	- Client overlay shows only available data (vicinity, velocity, mode)
116	- Client overlay explains that queue/target/motor are server-only
117	
118	v0.8.0: Bug 9 + Bug 10 — Stuck teleport fix + closest ore selection
119	- Bug 9: Replace Unstuck() teleport with physics-based escape (backward impulse + random torque)
120	- Bug 9: Add velocity-based stuck detection in Roam (catches AIMeEs pushing against each other)
121	- Bug 9: Increase obstacle raycast from 0.2m to 0.5m for earlier obstacle detection
122	- Bug 9: Randomize steering angle on reverse to break stuck loops
123	- Bug 10: Sort ore queue by distance — closest ore mined first
124	- Bug 10: Distance sorting applied before queue cap, so farthest ore is dropped
125	- Debug overlay now shows reversing state (Rev field)
126	
127	v0.7.0: Bug 8 — Carry collision launch fix
128	- Disable WheelColliders when AIMeE is being carried/dragged
129	- Prevents suspension spring and friction forces from launching the player
130	- Normal body collisions with walls/terrain still work as expected
131	- Zero residual motor/brake torque on pickup for clean drop behavior
132	- Call base DynamicThing.PhysicsUpdate for position tracking while carried
133	
134	v0.6.1: Silent operation by default
135	- Gate ALL runtime BepInEx logging behind aimeedebug toggle
136	- No console output unless debug overlay is explicitly enabled
137	- Startup/init messages still log once on load
138	
139	v0.6.0: Vicinity diagnostic overhaul
140	- Fix overlay showing different MineablesInVicinity than IC chip reads
141	- Overlay now reads via GetLogicValue (same path as IC) for consistency
142	- Add Bug7 call tracking: success/error counts visible in overlay
143	- Add GetLogicValue(MineablesInVicinity) diagnostic postfix logging
144	- Show both IC and direct vicinity values with MISMATCH detection
145	- Log vicinity count on mode changes for diagnosing premature returns
146	- Track vicinity changes in state change detection
147	- More frequent Bug7 diagnostics (every 5s instead of 10s)
148	- Always log Bug7 errors immediately (no more throttling)
149	
150	v0.5.0: Debug overlay system
151	- Add "aimeedebug" console command + F8 keyboard toggle
152	- Floating text above each AIMeE with stats (mode, queue, vicinity, target, etc.)
153	- Global debug panel (AIMeE count, AllAimeeQueuedMinables, time)
154	- State change detection with logging (mode, queue, mining, target, storage)
155	- Queue fill diagnostics with leak detection and emergency clear
156	- Mode 3 periodic logging every 0.5s
157	- Cached at 2Hz for minimal FPS impact
158	
159	v0.4.2: Queue estimate cap fix
160	- Cap MineablesInQueue client estimate to max 10 (queue cap)
161	
162	v0.4.1: MineablesInQueue client fallback restored
163	- Re-enable Bug 6 patch for MineablesInQueue on non-authority clients
164	- Return capped queue estimate (max 10) instead of hard-zero on clients
165	- Keep dedicated-server authority behavior unchanged (real queue still server-side)
166	
167	v0.4.0: Full Roam() overhaul + multi-AIMeE fairness
168	- Replace Bug 4 postfix with full Roam() prefix replacement
169	- Hybrid old/new flow: queue system + always-drive (never stops on empty queue)
170	- Cap queue to 10 per AIMeE to prevent AllAimeeQueuedMinables starvation
171	- Use exact game motor/brake formulas (RocketMath.MapToScale)
172	- Call original MovingToMineable() for mining state machine
173	- Mine at position using game's exact TryMineServer flow with queue dequeue
174	
175	v0.3.0: Bug 7 enabled, Bug 6 removed, roam fix
176	- Enable Bug 7 MineablesInVicinity bounds-check (was incorrectly disabled)
177	- Remove Bug 6 fake queue proxy that made MineablesInQueue == MineablesInVicinity
178	- Improve Bug 4 roam exploration: longer steering persistence (4-8s) to reduce circling
179	- Keep MinableSearchArea restored to 32 (original value when AIMeE worked well)
180	
181	v0.2.3: Bug 7 fix + Bug 6 ambiguous method fix
182	- Fix MineablesInVicinity overcounting ore outside search bounds (Bug 7)
183	- Fix GetLogicValue ambiguous match error from two-overload resolution (Bug 6)
184	
185	v0.2.2: Bug 6 fix
186	- Fix MineablesInQueue always 0 on multiplayer clients (Bug 6)
187	
188	v0.2.1: Bug 5 fix
189	- Fix picking up AIMeE launching player into the air (Bug 5)
190	- Changed UpdateEachFrame from prefix to postfix to preserve view-following
191	
192	v0.2.0: Bugs 3 and 4
193	- Fix tread UV animation on multiplayer clients via WheelRpm estimation from velocity (Bug 3)
194	- Fix server-side AnimateAuthority skipped when occluded (Bug 3)
195	- Fix Roam regression: restore driving logic when ore queue is empty (Bug 4)
196	- Restore MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32 (Bug 4)
197	
198	v0.1.0: Initial release
199	- Fix mine deadlock: _mineCancellation reset after Mine completes (Bug 1)
200	- Fix off-state teleporting: skip TryUnstuck/PathToTarget when off (Bug 2)
201	  ]]></ChangeLog>

207	  <InGameDescription><![CDATA[<size="30"><color=#ffa500>AIMeE Bug Fixes</color></size>
208	<color=#ff0000>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx with StationeersLaunchPad.</color>
209	
210	Fixes fourteen AIMeE mining robot bugs:
211	
212	<color=#ffa500>1. Mine Deadlock</color>
213	AIMeE stops after first ore because the mining cancellation token is never reset.
214	
215	<color=#ffa500>2. Off-State Teleporting</color>
216	When off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports due to missing power guards.
217	
218	<color=#ffa500>3. Tread Animation Desync</color>
219	Tread animations frozen on multiplayer clients. WheelRpm estimated from synced velocity.
220	
221	<color=#ffa500>4. Roam Regression</color>
222	AIMeE stops exploring when no ore nearby. Driving logic restored when queue empty. Scan range restored to 32.
223	
224	<color=#ffa500>5. Pickup Launch</color>
225	Picking up AIMeE launches the player. Motor/brakes zeroed while dragged.
226	
227	<color=#ffa500>6. MineablesInQueue (Multiplayer)</color>
228	Always 0 on clients. Uses terrain query as fallback for non-authority.
229	
230	<color=#ffa500>7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount</color>
231	Counts ore beyond search area. Added per-ore bounds check matching queue logic.
232	
233	<color=#ffa500>8. Carry Collision Launch</color>
234	Carrying AIMeE near terrain/objects launches player. WheelColliders disabled while carried.
235	
236	<color=#ffa500>9. Stuck Teleporting</color>
237	AIMeE teleports when stuck for 60s. Replaced with physics-based reverse + random turn.
238	
239	<color=#ffa500>10. Distant Ore Selection</color>
240	AIMeE mines far-away ore first. Queue now sorted by distance — closest ore mined first.
241	
242	<color=#ffa500>11. Circling Fix</color>
243	AIMeEs circle aimlessly due to stuck detection feedback loop. Post-reverse cooldown and steering reset break the cycle. Queue cap reduced for multi-AIMeE fairness.
244	
245	<color=#ffa500>12. Ghost Ore Fix (Multiplayer)</color>
246	Ore mined by AIMeE stays visible on clients. Fixed by injecting sync event directly — client removes the ore model. Zero terrain modification.
247	
248	<color=#ffa500>13. Underground Ore Fix</color>
249	AIMeE targets ore deep underground in tunnels/caves. Column scan now detects cave ceilings and rejects unreachable ore.
250	
251	<color=#ffa500>14. Stale Queue Fix</color>
252	AIMeE's ore queue isn't cleared on pickup, power-on, or TargetXYZ set. Stale entries waste time and block other AIMeEs. Queue now resets on relocation.
253	
254	<color=#888888>Debug overlay shows [SERVER]/[REMOTE] labels. On dedicated server clients, server-only values (queue, target, motor) display as unavailable.</color>
255	]]></InGameDescription>
256	</ModMetadata>
257
```

```text
11	
12	    Adds an inspector and debugger for IC10 chips. Readout includes current values for registers and stack. Updated every tick.
13	    The debugger can frame-step IC10 programs and also shows current code-line and the fiew surrounding it.
14	    The Debug state is not saved on world save, any paused/slowed chips will run at full speed on loading a save. So, if you have code that you are unsure of will cause mayhem if "started", stop the housing properly before saving.
15	    
16	    This is the FlorpyDorp edit of Roze's IC10 Inspector with a dedicated server networking fix.
17	    Now works on dedicated servers — clients properly receive and display IC10 chip data.
18	
19	    v0.3.7 (FlorpyDorp edit)
20	    * Fixed: Dedicated server clients now see IC10 data on computer screens and tablet cartridges
21	    * Root cause: CartridgeTick only runs server-side, so ReadProcessorState never fired on clients
22	
23	    v0.3
24	    * If you have subscribed and enabled [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3528329094]IC10Extender mod[/url], you can now use the newly added break command to halt the processor at a given line. Unfortunately this requires a precious code line, but it is what it is. If you decide to add IC10Extender, [b]Make sure it loads BEFORE mine[/b]
```

```text
11			[h1]Modular Console Mod[/h1]

14			This mod adds [b]modular console structures[/b] that let you build real, customizable control panels.
15	
16			Each console base connects with others and holds [b]device boards[/b]—predefined layouts where you can install a wide range of [b]input and readout devices[/b]. Devices can be freely repositioned with a screwdriver, so you can tweak your setup without rebuilding everything.
17	
18			Whether you're organizing a control station, managing access points, or just want a proper home for your buttons and lights—this mod brings [b]structure[/b], [b]clarity[/b], and [b]flexibility[/b] to your logic layouts.
```

```text
13	_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

16	Based on my research, here's what I found about the IC10 editor's dark bordered area:
17	
18	## Research Summary
19	
20	### How the IC10 Editor Dark Area is Rendered
21	
22	The dark bordered area uses a **simple 2-layer sprite technique**:
23	
24	1. **Outer Layer**: `dialog-bg.png` (9-slice sprite, 18px borders) with dark blue color `#002A47FF`
25	2. **Inner Layer**: **The SAME `dialog-bg.png` sprite** but colored BLACK at 75% opacity `#000000BF`
26	
27	The "border" effect comes from **inset positioning** - the inner dark layer is offset ~6px from the outer blue layer, so the blue shows through as a visible border.
28	
29	### Key Discovery
30
```

```text
17	5. I want there to be a nested file heirarchy system on the left side of it where you can make new files and categorize them/nest them. So I can make a "Build the base" file and inside I can have "Airlock" "Hydroponics" for my own note taking.
```

 `C:\Dev\StationpediaAscendedWorkspace\DllCompare.cs`

```text
1	using System;
2	using System.IO;
3	using System.Linq;
4	using System.Reflection;
5	using System.Collections.Generic;
6	
7	public class DllCompare {
8	    public static void Main(string[] args) {
9	        var origPath = args[0];
10	        var ourPath = args[1];
11	        
12	        var runtimeDir = System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeEnvironment.GetRuntimeDirectory();
13	        var resolver = new PathAssemblyResolver(Directory.GetFiles(runtimeDir, "*.dll").Concat(new[]{origPath}).ToArray());
14	        
15	        using var mlcOrig = new MetadataLoadContext(resolver);
16	        var origAsm = mlcOrig.LoadFromAssemblyPath(origPath);
17	        
18	        var resolver2 = new PathAssemblyResolver(Directory.GetFiles(runtimeDir, "*.dll").Concat(new[]{ourPath}).ToArray());
19	        using var mlcOur = new MetadataLoadContext(resolver2);
20	        var ourAsm = mlcOur.LoadFromAssemblyPath(ourPath);
21	        
22	        // Compare DebugMotherboard type
23	        var origType = origAsm.GetTypes().FirstOrDefault(t => t.Name == "DebugMotherboard");
24	        var ourType = ourAsm.GetTypes().FirstOrDefault(t => t.Name == "DebugMotherboard");
25	        
26	        if (origType == null) { Console.WriteLine("DebugMotherboard not in original!"); return; }
27	        if (ourType == null) { Console.WriteLine("DebugMotherboard not in ours!"); return; }
28	        
29	        // Get all fields
30	        var origFields = origType.GetFields(BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic | BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Static)
31	            .Select(f => f.Name).OrderBy(n => n).ToList();
32	        var ourFields = ourType.GetFields(BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic | BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Static)
33	            .Select(f => f.Name).OrderBy(n => n).ToList();
34	        
35	        var onlyOrig = origFields.Except(ourFields).ToList();
36	        var onlyOurs = ourFields.Except(origFields).ToList();
37	        
38	        Console.WriteLine($"Original field count: {origFields.Count}");
39	        Console.WriteLine($"Our field count: {ourFields.Count}");
40	        
41	        if (onlyOrig.Any()) {
42	            Console.WriteLine("\nFields ONLY in original:");
43	            foreach (var f in onlyOrig) Console.WriteLine($"  - {f}");
44	        }
45	        if (onlyOurs.Any()) {
46	            Console.WriteLine("\nFields ONLY in ours:");
47	            foreach (var f in onlyOurs) Console.WriteLine($"  + {f}");
48	        }
49	        
50	        // Also compare all types
51	        var origTypes = origAsm.GetTypes().Select(t => t.FullName).OrderBy(n => n).ToList();
52	        var ourTypes = ourAsm.GetTypes().Select(t => t.FullName).OrderBy(n => n).ToList();
53	        
54	        var onlyOrigTypes = origTypes.Except(ourTypes).ToList();
55	        var onlyOurTypes = ourTypes.Except(origTypes).ToList();
56	        
57	        if (onlyOrigTypes.Any()) {
58	            Console.WriteLine("\nTypes ONLY in original:");
59	            foreach (var t in onlyOrigTypes) Console.WriteLine($"  - {t}");
60	        }
61	        if (onlyOurTypes.Any()) {
62	            Console.WriteLine("\nTypes ONLY in ours:");
63	            foreach (var t in onlyOurTypes) Console.WriteLine($"  + {t}");
64	        }
65	    }
66	}
67
```

```text
31	_**Agent (copilot/claude-haiku-4.5)**_
32	
33	I provided that diagnosis (I'm Claude Haiku). Here's a quick summary:
34	
35	## Stationeers Netcode Bugs Causing LA→NZ Desync
36	
37	**Root causes:**
38	
39	1. **Sequential fragment reassembly with no out-of-order tolerance** — Game state is split into 1024-byte fragments. If any arrive out of order over the transpacific route, the entire state tick corrupts or is lost. VPN masks this by providing ordered delivery.
40	
41	2. **Fragment overload** — 20 state ticks/second × potentially 50–200+ fragments each. Client can only process 50 fragments/frame. Reordering cascades into backlog.
42	
43	3. **60-second time sync** — Client clock only corrects once per minute with a hard jump. Desync measured in minutes because the clock drifts for 60 seconds before any correction.
44	
45	4. **GZip Optimal compression on join** — Slow compression stalls the server's tick loop when new players join, causing mid-session packet loss for existing players.
46	
47	**Why the VPN "fixes" it:** Routes through a single cable with low jitter and consistent packet ordering. Fragments arrive sequentially, reassembly works, no cascading failures.
48	
49	**Fix:** Add sequence numbers to fragments, lerp time sync every 5–10 seconds, use faster compression.
50	
51	---
```

**After I COPY each foreign mod out to its own new C:\Dev folder (+ its specstory chats), how should I handle the ORIGINAL copies still sitting in this workspace?**

Options:
- **Move originals to in-workspace _TRASH** - Copy out to new workspaces, then MOVE the originals into a new _TRASH_REVIEW folder here. Cleans the workspace immediately; you delete _TRASH when comfortable. Nothing is ever hard-deleted by me.
- **Copy only, leave originals in place** - Make the new workspaces but leave everything here untouched. Safest, but the workspace stays cluttered until you manually remove things.

**There's already a C:\Dev\"IC10 Git Repository" folder, and StationeersIC10Inspector has its own .git repo. Where should the IC10 Inspector cluster (3 mod folders, DLLs, patches, DllCompare, zip) go?**

Options:
- **New 'IC10Inspector Workspace' folder** - Create a fresh C:\Dev\IC10Inspector Workspace and copy the whole cluster there together. Keeps it self-contained; you can reconcile with the existing repo later.
- **Merge into existing 'IC10 Git Repository'** - Copy the cluster into the existing C:\Dev\IC10 Git Repository folder. Risk: may collide with what's already there; I'd nest it under a subfolder to avoid overwriting.

**How should I treat shared reference / third-party material that isn't really part of the Stationpedia mod?** _(select multiple)_

Options:
- **Keep game source & BepInEx here** - Leave StationeersGameVersions (game code, used for research) and BepInEx.Debug-r11 in the SPA workspace as reference. They're useful across all your mods.
- **Modular Consoles MOD → its own/new folder** - It's a third-party mod by other authors. Copy it out to C:\Dev\Modular Consoles MOD (or _TRASH) rather than leaving it in the SPA workspace.
- **Loose saves/logs → _TRASH** - Treat world.xml (12MB test save), LOGS.txt, search*.txt, and the two Unity temp projects as disposable clutter to stage in _TRASH.

**How far should I go reorganizing the Stationpedia Ascended mod ITSELF (the part that stays)?**

Options:
- **Conservative — don't touch shipping mod folders** - Only tidy loose root-level dev files into folders (e.g. _dev-notes, _logs). Leave StationpediaAscended/ and its About/, mod/, assets/ exactly as-is to avoid breaking the mod load.
- **Tidy loose files + light internal grouping** - Also group loose docs/dev-only files inside StationpediaAscended (e.g. move dev .txt notes into a docs/ subfolder), but never touch About/, mod/, assets/, or anything the game loads.


**Answer:** Move originals to in-workspace _TRASH, That folder should go into a "IC10 Inspector Network Fix Workspace" folder of its own, Keep it here but make a folder called "Reference", Tidy loose files + light internal grouping

```md
# Workspace Cleanup Plan — Stationpedia Ascended

## Context

`c:\Dev\StationpediaAscendedWorkspace` is the workspace for the **Stationpedia Ascended** mod (Stationeers), but over time several *unrelated* mods and a lot of loose dev clutter got dumped into the same folder and the same git repo: a NetFix mod, an AIMeE bug-fix mod, a whole IC10 Inspector cluster (3 mod folders + DLLs + patches + a diff tool), a third-party Modular Consoles mod, game-source reference snapshots, a 12 MB test save, logs, and ~100 SpecStory chat transcripts spanning all of these.

Goal: give each foreign mod its **own workspace folder** under `C:\Dev` (with the SpecStory conversations that belong to it, so context carries over), corral shared reference material into a `Reference/` folder, stage everything destined for removal in an in-workspace `_TRASH_REVIEW/` folder, and lightly tidy the Stationpedia Ascended mod itself — **without deleting anything** and **without breaking any functional mod**.

### Hard rules (from the user)
- **NOTHING is hard-deleted.** Removal candidates are *moved* into `_TRASH_REVIEW/`; the user deletes them later once comfortable.
- Foreign mods are **copied out** to new `C:\Dev` folders; originals are then **moved** into `_TRASH_REVIEW/`.
- Never touch the shipping mod payload (`About/`, `mod/`, `assets/`, prefabs) of any mod.

### Key facts found
- The whole workspace is **one git repo**. `StationeersIC10Inspector/` has its **own nested `.git`** (plain dir in parent, not a submodule — no `.gitmodules`). `NetFix/` and `world.xml` are **untracked**. `StationeersGameVersions/` and all `bin/`/`obj/`/`build/` are **gitignored**.
- Moving tracked items into `_TRASH_REVIEW/` (same repo) shows in git as **renames, not deletions** — history is preserved. I will **not commit**; the user commits when ready.

---

## Decisions (confirmed with user)
1. **Originals** → after copying out, move into in-workspace `_TRASH_REVIEW/`.
2. **IC10 cluster** → new folder `C:\Dev\IC10 Inspector Network Fix Workspace\`.
3. **Reference/third-party material** → keep in the workspace under a new `Reference/` folder (not trashed, not new workspace).
4. **SPA mod** → tidy loose root files + light internal grouping; never touch `About/`, `mod/`, `assets/`.

---

## Part 1 — Extract foreign mods to new C:\Dev workspaces (COPY)

Copy with hidden files + nested `.git` preserved (robocopy `/E` or `cp -r`). Build artifacts (`bin/obj/build`) are copied too so each new workspace is immediately buildable.

### A. `C:\Dev\NetFix Workspace\`
- Copy `NetFix/` (currently untracked).
- Copy these SpecStory chats into `NetFix Workspace\specstory\`:
  - `2026-03-18_04-06-24Z-bug-report-for-regenerate-rooms-command-in-stationeers.md`
  - `2026-06-03_20-15-46Z-please-take-a-look.md`

### B. `C:\Dev\AimeeBugFix Workspace\`
- Copy `AimeeBugFixes/`.
- Copy into `AimeeBugFix Workspace\specstory\`:
  - `2026-02-07_01-43-13Z-debugging-aimees-multiplayer-behavior-issues.md` (+ its `_01-43Z` duplicate)

### C. `C:\Dev\IC10 Inspector Network Fix Workspace\`
Copy the whole IC10 cluster together:
- `StationeersIC10Inspector/` (preserve its nested `.git`)
- `3508602436/`  and  `3675848606/` (built workshop versions)
- `IC10Inspector_ORIG.dll`, `IC10Inspector_OURS.dll`
- `DllCompare/`, `DllCompare.cs`
- `StationeersIC10Inspector.zip`
- patches: `about_fix.patch`, `cartridge_fix.patch`, `inspector_fix.patch`, `modmessage_fix.patch`, `motherboard_fix.patch`
- Copy into `…\specstory\`:
  - `2026-01-05_04-07-25Z-ic10-editor-dark-border-rendering-issue.md` (+ `_04-07Z` dup)
  - `2026-02-28_07-28-47Z-mod-repo-pull-and-debugging-request.md`

> SpecStory originals are **copied, not moved** — the SPA workspace keeps its full chat history.

---

## Part 2 — `Reference/` folder (stays in workspace)

Create `Reference/` and move into it:
- `Modular Consoles MOD/` (third-party mod, by other authors — reference only)
- `StationeersGameVersions/` (game-source snapshots; stays gitignored — the ignore pattern matches by folder name regardless of location)
- `BepInEx.Debug-r11/` (framework reference)

---

## Part 3 — `_TRASH_REVIEW/` folder (staging for user to delete later)

Create `_TRASH_REVIEW/` and move into it:
- **Originals of the extracted mods** (after Part 1 copy verified): `NetFix/`, `AimeeBugFixes/`, and every IC10-cluster item listed in 1C.
- **Disposable clutter:** `world.xml` (12 MB test save), `LOGS.txt`, `search_results.txt`, `search2.txt`, `search3.txt`, `search4.txt`, `build_output/` (regenerated on build).
- **Unity temp projects — FLAGGED:** `TableCell Stationpedia Ascended/`, `Tmp_Input Stationpedia Ascended/`. These look like throwaway prefab-authoring scratch projects. Staged in trash, but **verify you don't need them to re-author UI prefabs** before deleting.

A short `_TRASH_REVIEW/README.txt` will list what each item is and where its copy/replacement lives, so deletion later is informed.

---

## Part 4 — Light tidy of Stationpedia Ascended (the part that stays)

Only safe, reversible grouping. **Do not touch** `About/`, `mod/`, `assets/`, `announcements/`, `Unity Prefabs/`, `logic-documentation/`, or any folder the game loads.

- Inside `StationpediaAscended/`, create `_dev/` and move clearly dev-only loose files into it:
  - `StationpediaAscended_LLM_Instructions.txt`
  - `VERSION-LOCATIONS.txt`
  - `PHASE2_IMPLEMENTATION.md`
  - `release_posts.md`
- Leave `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `CONFIGURATION.md`, `.gitignore`, the `.vdf` workshop files, and all subfolders **as-is**.
- **Guard:** before moving, grep `About/About.xml` (and `mod/`) to confirm none of these filenames are referenced at load time. If any is referenced, leave it in place.

### Resulting clean workspace root
`.claude/  .git/  .gitignore  .specstory/  .vscode/  StationpediaAscended/  StationpediaEditor/  Reference/  _TRASH_REVIEW/`

(`StationpediaEditor/` — the companion editor app — stays; it's part of the SPA workflow.)

---

## Execution order
1. Create the 3 new `C:\Dev` workspace folders + their `specstory/` subfolders.
2. **Copy** foreign mods and their chats out (Part 1). **Verify copies** (file counts / key files present, nested `.git` intact).
3. Create `Reference/`, move reference material in (Part 2).
4. Create `_TRASH_REVIEW/` + README, move originals & clutter in (Part 3).
5. Light SPA tidy with the About.xml guard (Part 4).
6. Report final `git status` and the new tree; **do not commit** (user commits when ready).

## Verification
- New workspaces: confirm each contains the mod's `.csproj`/`About.xml` and `specstory/*.md`; for IC10 confirm `StationeersIC10Inspector/.git` survived the copy (`git -C "…\StationeersIC10Inspector" log -1`).
- SPA mod still loads: confirm `StationpediaAscended/About/About.xml` and `StationpediaAscended/mod/` are untouched; `About.xml` does not reference any moved `_dev/` file.
- Repo integrity: `git status` shows moved tracked items as **renames** (R), no unexpected deletions; `git fsck` clean.
- Nothing missing: every original still exists somewhere (new workspace, `Reference/`, or `_TRASH_REVIEW/`) — confirmed by a before/after top-level inventory diff.
```

```bash
done < /tmp/before_root.txt
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-19 04:22:08Z)**_

You're absolutely right — those are **not** disposable, and my "disposable clutter" instinct was wrong (which is exactly why I flagged them instead of assuming). Looking inside, they're the **Unity source projects that author Stationpedia Ascended's UI**:

- **TableCell** → `Assets/Prefabs/Tables/` holds the source `.prefab` files for the table feature (TableCell, TableContainer, TableHeaderRow, TableRow, TableSeparator).
- **Tmp_Input** → `Assets/Prefabs/UI/SA_TMPInputField_Base.prefab` plus `Assets/Editor/BuildAssetBundle.cs` and `Assets/AssetBundles/stationpediaascended_ui` — i.e. this is the project that **builds the `stationpediaascended_ui` asset bundle your mod ships**.

So these are real editable source for the mod. The 1.7 GB is almost entirely Unity's regenerable `Library/` cache — the actual tracked source in `Assets/` is only ~4 MB each (and `Library/` is already gitignored).

Let me pull them back out of trash and keep them, grouped into a clear `Unity Projects/` folder at the workspace root (they're build-time source, not part of the shipped mod payload):

```bash
cd /c/Dev/StationpediaAscendedWorkspace
mkdir -p "Unity Projects"

  if mv "_TRASH_REVIEW/$d" "Unity Projects/" 2>/dev/null; then echo "$d -> Unity Projects/  OK"
  else echo "$d : move failed (likely Unity/locked) — retrying via powershell"; fi
done
echo ""

ls -1 _TRASH_REVIEW | grep -iE "tablecell|tmp_input" || echo "(neither still in trash — good)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-19 04:22:47Z)**_

Good catch — thank you. You were right and they were not disposable.

**What they actually are:** the Unity source projects behind your mod's UI:
- **TableCell** = source `.prefab`s for the Stationpedia tables.
- **Tmp_Input** = the TMP input-field prefab plus `Assets/Editor/BuildAssetBundle.cs`, the script that **builds the `stationpediaascended_ui` asset bundle your mod ships**. Losing this would mean losing the ability to rebuild your UI bundle.

**Fixed:**
- Moved both **out of `_TRASH_REVIEW/` into a new `Unity Projects/` folder** at the workspace root (kept, not trashed). Git tracks them there now (shown as renames).
- Updated `_TRASH_REVIEW/README.txt` to record that they were rescued.

**Workspace root now:** `Reference/  StationpediaAscended/  StationpediaEditor/  Unity Projects/  _TRASH_REVIEW/` (+ dotfiles).

One optional note: each of those Unity projects is ~1.7 GB, but that's almost entirely Unity's `Library/` cache, which regenerates automatically when you open the project and is already gitignored. If you ever want to reclaim space you can safely delete the `Library/` folders — but nothing needs doing now.

Anything else look off, or want me to double-check any other folder I staged into `_TRASH_REVIEW/`?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-19 04:24:22Z)**_

Good instinct to ask — here's the full picture.

**The Unity projects you asked about: no, nothing to change.** The build doesn't reference them. `StationpediaAscended.csproj` copies the asset bundle from `$(ProjectDir)stationpediaascended_ui` — i.e. the **pre-built** `StationpediaAscended/mod/stationpediaascended_ui` (still there, 364 KB, untouched). The `TableCell`/`Tmp_Input` Unity projects are only where you *author and rebuild* that bundle; no task or csproj points at their path. The only connection is your manual step of copying a freshly-built bundle out of the Unity project into `mod/` — you'll just grab it from `Unity Projects/…` now instead of the root. Build is unaffected.

**But there's real fallout you may not have spotted:** [tasks.json](.vscode/tasks.json) contains build/package/publish tasks for the mods I *extracted*, and their `cwd` now points into `_TRASH_REVIEW/`:
- **AimeeBugFixes** — 3 tasks (lines 63–116) → `${workspaceFolder}/AimeeBugFixes`
- **IC10Inspector** — 3 tasks (lines 117–171) → `${workspaceFolder}/StationeersIC10Inspector`
- **NetFix** — 2 tasks (lines 172–203) → `${workspaceFolder}/NetFix`

Those 8 tasks are now broken in this workspace. Since each of those mods now has its own workspace, the clean fix is to **move those task definitions into each new workspace's `.vscode/tasks.json`** (with `cwd` repointed) and **trim them out of this one**, leaving only the 4 Stationpedia Ascended tasks here.

The Stationpedia tasks themselves are all fine — they point at `StationpediaAscended/` and `StationpediaAscended/mod`, which didn't move.

Want me to do that task migration now? I'd:
1. Create `.vscode/tasks.json` in each of the 3 new workspaces with just that mod's tasks (cwd adjusted to the new workspace root).
2. Remove those 8 tasks from this workspace's `tasks.json`, keeping the 4 SPA ones.
3. Leave the originals in `_TRASH_REVIEW/` untouched (nothing deleted).

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-19 05:41:52Z)**_

Good news — all three extracted mods are **fully self-contained**. Nothing shared got left behind, and they'll build standalone in their new workspaces.

Here's what I confirmed by reading each `.csproj`:

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `<Import>` of an external/shared `.props`/`.targets` | **None** — no project imports anything outside its own folder |
| Game-DLL path source | **Hardcoded inline in each csproj** |
| Env-var dependency (e.g. a global `StationeersManagedPath`) | **None** — the variable is *defined* in each csproj, not read from the environment |
| HintPaths escaping the mod folder (`..\..` to siblings) | **None** — all point at the absolute Stationeers install or the project's own files |
| Root-level `Directory.Build.props` / `global.json` / `nuget.config` being inherited | **None existed** at this workspace's root, so nothing was being inherited to lose |

Each project independently declares the same line near the top, e.g.:
```xml
<StationeersPath>C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers</StationeersPath>
<StationeersManagedPath>$(StationeersPath)\rocketstation_Data\Managed</StationeersManagedPath>
<BepInExPath>$(StationeersPath)\BepInEx\core</BepInExPath>
```
…and references everything via those (IC10 uses `StationeersDir`/`ManagedDir`, same idea). So the **only** build requirement is that Stationeers stays installed at that Steam path — which is unchanged on your machine. I also checked `C:\Dev\` itself: no `Directory.Build.props`/`global.json` there either, so moving the mods one level up changes nothing about MSBuild inheritance.

**Bottom line:** no config needs to be copied or fixed — `dotnet build` will work in each new workspace as-is. The only mild downside is the hardcoded absolute path (if you ever move your Steam library, you'd edit each csproj), but that was already true before the reorg.

That wraps up everything you asked: workspace cleaned, foreign mods extracted with their chats, reference material corralled, Unity source rescued, trash staged for your review, and build tasks split per-workspace.
